action.text: let varName: <string, number, boolean, string[], number[]> = <value>;

let data: any // Compiler will skip type checking. Use this if the type is dynamic and unknown now
let data: unknown // More type-safety. Use this for data from external sources(APIs, user input...))

let varName: <type> | undefined // Type of varName can be <type> or undefined, which means, this variable might not be
assigned with a value yet, this will be done later (e.g. by ngOnInit)
let varName: <type> | null = null // Use this if the variable is actively cleared or reset during runtime
user!.name // Tell the compiler that user is absolutly not empty
const value = input ?? 'default' // Assign to 'default' if value is null or undefined
const street = user?.address?.street; // Safely access nested properties
